Package com.yanimetaxas.realitycheck

Interface Check<SELF extends Check<SELF,ACTUAL>,ACTUAL>

Type Parameters:
SELF - the self-referencing type for fluent chaining
ACTUAL - the type of the value under test
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractCheck, AbstractTemporalCheck, ArrayCheck, BigDecimalCheck, BigIntegerCheck, BooleanCheck, ByteArrayCheck, CollectionCheck, ComparableCheck, CsvCheck, DoubleArrayCheck, DurationCheck, EnumCheck, ExecutionCheck, FileCheck, FutureCheck, InputStreamCheck, InstantCheck, IntArrayCheck, IterableCheck, JsonCheck, LocalDateCheck, LocalDateTimeCheck, LongArrayCheck, MapCheck, MatchResultCheck, MultilineCheck, NumberCheck, ObjectCheck, OffsetDateTimeCheck, OptionalCheck, OrderedCheck, SealedClassCheck, SnapshotCheck, StreamCheck, StringCheck, ThrowableCheck, UriCheck, UuidCheck, XmlCheck, YamlCheck, ZonedDateTimeCheck
public interface Check<SELF extends Check<SELF,ACTUAL>,ACTUAL>
Core check interface. All assertion types implement this.

Built-in checks extend AbstractCheck. Custom checks can implement this interface directly (including as records) for zero-boilerplate extension: 


 public record MoneyCheck(Money actual, FailureHandler failureHandler)
         implements Check<MoneyCheck, Money> {
     @Override public MoneyCheck self() { return this; }

     public MoneyCheck hasCurrency(String code) {
         return failureHandler.check(self(),
             actual.getCurrency().equals(code),
             "expected currency <%s> but was <%s>", code, actual.getCurrency());
     }
 }

      default SELF isInstanceOf(Class<?> type)

    • satisfies

      default SELF satisfies(Predicate<ACTUAL> condition, String description)

    • matches

      default SELF matches(Predicate<ACTUAL> condition)
      Asserts that the value matches the given predicate. Alias for satisfies(Predicate, String) with a generic message.

    • satisfies

      default SELF satisfies(Consumer<ACTUAL> assertions)
      Passes the actual value to the given consumer for arbitrary inline assertions. Useful for one-off checks that don't warrant a custom check class. 
      
 checkThat(person).satisfies(p -> {
     checkThat(p.name()).isNotEmpty();
     checkThat(p.age()).isPositive();
 });
